codeReview.summary: Implementation adds ConfidenceLevel enum with 4 threshold zones (Deprecated<0.3, Uncertain 0.3-0.6, Probable 0.6-0.8, Confident>0.8), BetaPosterior.confidence_level() method, Procedure.confidence_level() method, has_sufficient_data() and total_observations() helpers, with 11 comprehensive tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed all established project conventions. The implementation uses existing patterns: async_trait for async traits, Arc<dyn Trait> for dynamic dispatch, thiserror::Error for error types, and #[tokio::test] for async tests as documented in AGENTS.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: procedural.rs follows the same error handling patterns (Result<T, SwellError>) and struct naming conventions (PascalCase) as other modules in swell-memory

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the Rust skill procedures. The skillFeedback indicates the procedure was followed correctly. Implementation uses standard Rust patterns for Bayesian inference with Beta posterior distribution.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker loaded Rust skill before implementation, followed by standard cargo build/test/clippy workflow

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used standard commands (cargo build, cargo test, cargo clippy) that are all documented in services.yaml. No deviation or additional services needed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verification shows cargo build --workspace, cargo test commands, and cargo clippy were used - all match services.yaml commands

sharedStateObservations.observation: No knowledge gaps identified. Worker correctly leveraged existing BetaPosterior structure which already had alpha/beta tracking and update() method. The new ConfidenceLevel is a straightforward addition using the mean() method of BetaPosterior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Existing codebase had BetaPosterior with alpha/beta tracking at lines 220-400 in procedural.rs. Worker correctly identified that only ConfidenceLevel enum and confidence_level() methods were needed

summary: Implementation passes review. ConfidenceLevel enum with 4 threshold zones is correctly implemented using Beta posterior mean. The thresholds <0.3 (Deprecated), 0.3-0.6 (Uncertain), 0.6-0.8 (Probable), >0.8 (Confident) are correctly implemented via from_probability() and confidence_level() methods. BetaPosterior.confidence_level() and Procedure.confidence_level() provide per-memory confidence queries. 11 tests verify threshold boundaries, zone transitions, serialization, and helper methods. No blocking issues found.
